title: Allow multiple containers and all for umount
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Allow the capability to umount one to n containers or to umount all mounted containers at once.  Based on the recent Buildah PR:  https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/825

Test output:

```
 podman mount cf65022366da
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ddc2d608f62f3b3f8f71122335812dfe5a3ec28d4feec8dd0e63ed416948250e/merged
# podman mount 913228bb56b2
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/a2352119c4dd3ed83da832ce06d1f0f23623355a591b17dff50fc3b669377bd8/merged

# podman umount --all
913228bb56b27979414108dbe91a2c348cca480f6267fee592fb9556888bd6d9
cf65022366da87138803cb399130a6c1ac3c2ddd82f82b23190a5b602b631f60

# podman mount cf65022366da
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ddc2d608f62f3b3f8f71122335812dfe5a3ec28d4feec8dd0e63ed416948250e/merged
# podman mount 913228bb56b2
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/a2352119c4dd3ed83da832ce06d1f0f23623355a591b17dff50fc3b669377bd8/merged

# podman umount cf65022366da 913228bb56b2
cf65022366da87138803cb399130a6c1ac3c2ddd82f82b23190a5b602b631f60
913228bb56b27979414108dbe91a2c348cca480f6267fee592fb9556888bd6d9

# podman mount cf65022366da
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ddc2d608f62f3b3f8f71122335812dfe5a3ec28d4feec8dd0e63ed416948250e/merged
# podman mount 913228bb56b2
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/a2352119c4dd3ed83da832ce06d1f0f23623355a591b17dff50fc3b669377bd8/merged

# podman umount cf65022366da badd 913228bb56b2 not here
cf65022366da87138803cb399130a6c1ac3c2ddd82f82b23190a5b602b631f60
913228bb56b27979414108dbe91a2c348cca480f6267fee592fb9556888bd6d9
ERRO[0000] error unmounting container badd: no container with name or ID badd found: no such container
error unmounting container badd: no container with name or ID badd found: no such container
ERRO[0000] error unmounting container not: no container with name or ID not found: no such container
error unmounting container not: no container with name or ID not found: no such container
ERRO[0000] error unmounting container here: no container with name or ID here found: no such container
error unmounting container here: no container with name or ID here found: no such container

```

comments.body:
LGTM
@baude @mheon @umohnani8 @giuseppe @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
LGTM. Thanks @TomSweeneyRedHat !

My waffling for this and the one on Buildah is printing out the error lines like:

```
ERRO[0000] error unmounting container not: no container with name or ID not found: no such container
```
I'm thinking we might want drop the logrus.error calls, happy to hear other opinions.

Once this gets merged, I'll work on adding the multi container mounts, that too was recently added to Buildah.
Can you add a check to see if it is mounted before attempting an unmount?

I'm a bit confused as to why we want this. Unmount all is going to fail for
running containers, and only really makes sense with podman mount, so I
don't know if I see much of a point to this.

It might also conflict with the mount sessions work I'd eventually like to
get merged.

On Wed, Jun 27, 2018, 14:12 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> if mounted, _ := ctr.Mounted(); mounted {
> ctr.Unmont()
> }
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1012#issuecomment-400779115>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCPvlFdIHj0HtAF4EK9dnHHwbFC1Pks5uA8sJgaJpZM4U6Hp4>
> .
>

The root of this came from the Buildah test folks.  They just found it a lot easier to be able to remove the mounts from all the containers rather than specifying one at a time.  If I were able to add a check to see if the container was running and ignore it if so, would that be better?

I don't know if it helps or not with the session work, but I didn't remove the ability to remove one container at a time.

I'd feel a lot better if we only allowed containers that are not running to
be unmounted, sure.

I think we can avoid conflicts with sessions too, on second thought.

On Wed, Jun 27, 2018, 15:04 Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> The root of this came from the Buildah test folks. They just found it a
> lot easier to be able to remove the mounts from all the containers rather
> than specifying one at a time. If I were able to add a check to see if the
> container was running and ignore it is so, would that be better?
>
> I don't know if it helps or not with the session work, but I didn't remove
> the ability to remove one container at a time.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1012#issuecomment-400794829>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCPyVuqtuqzfbjPNqNVTQOtbPJiL1ks5uA9dEgaJpZM4U6Hp4>
> .
>

Updated to hopefully address @mheon and @rhatdan 's comments.  I'd like to remove the logrus.Error  calls, but think someone wanted them.  If they remain, we get double output of the error.  The line with ERRO is the one generated from that call.  For example:

```
# podman umount cf65022366da bad 772145f69401
cf65022366da87138803cb399130a6c1ac3c2ddd82f82b23190a5b602b631f60
772145f69401b2cb9e56c5e36ddf2fa7e398791bdb210b4910f893e33a0e5014
ERRO[0000] error unmounting container bad: no container with name or ID bad found: no such container 
error unmounting container bad: no container with name or ID bad found: no such container
```

Thoughts?
@TomSweeneyRedHat You had one extra logrus.Error, getting rid of that should fix it

Repushed.  I kept the logrus with the exception of the last one and trashed the write utility.  Error output now looks like:
```
# podman umount 6bc14f73db9f bad1 8c046b5cbfe0 bad2 772145f69401 bad3 cf65022366da
6bc14f73db9fee537724da299e76bf42610744464237007664a6faa7c114085b
8c046b5cbfe090391f562b02bff68e1705f954b42f7cec8640c757d1d6433c3f
ERRO[0000] error unmounting container bad1: no container with name or ID bad1 found: no such container 
772145f69401b2cb9e56c5e36ddf2fa7e398791bdb210b4910f893e33a0e5014
ERRO[0000] error unmounting container bad2: no container with name or ID bad2 found: no such container 
cf65022366da87138803cb399130a6c1ac3c2ddd82f82b23190a5b602b631f60
error unmounting container bad3: no container with name or ID bad3 found: no such container
```
The last error doesn't look like the others, I think that's OK.  It does get the message across.
LGTM
LGTM
LGTM. Let's merge once tests green.

@rh-atomic-bot r+
@mheon When you want to merge on green, just do the above command.  Humu will block if a test fails, Then you don't have to remember to come back and merge it later.
